reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: 'i' is undeclared in this scope. In C99 and later, declaring 'int i' in the previous for-loop limits its scope to that loop. It needs to be declared at the beginning of the function or re-declared here.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: 'i' is undeclared in this scope. In C90 or if not declared at the beginning of the block/function, 'i' must be declared. The previous loop used 'int i', which is scoped to that loop. This loop needs its own declaration or a shared declaration.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: 'i' is not declared in this scope. The loop uses 'i' but it was declared in the earlier 'for' loop which is out of scope now (C90 standard). Declare 'int i;' before this loop.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [variable 'i' is not declared in this scope; the loop uses 'i' but it was declared in the earlier for-loop which is out of scope in C89/C90]
